description: Long-term episodic memory for OpenClaw agents. Uses a Sequential Cache-and-Drain architecture to safely split massive chat logs into 64K chunks, then narrativizes them into continuous episodes via OpenRouter — with per-agent continuity, adaptive idle backoff, wake() guarantees, crash-safe durability, and configurable reasoning/thinking control for higher output quality.

GEMINI_API_KEY.description: Gemini API key used by the Go sidecar to embed conversation episodes via the Gemini Embedding API. This key must already be present in your OpenClaw environment — the plugin reads it from the environment and never stores it.

enabled.description:
ON/OFF switch for the Episodic Memory engine. Default: true.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If OFF: The AI completely loses its long-term memory and acts like a goldfish with complete amnesia.

reserveTokens.description:
The maximum 'brain space' (tokens) reserved strictly for injecting past memories into the system prompt. Default: 2048.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: The AI's prompt gets too crowded, leaving no room to process your current question (causing crashes). If too low: The AI becomes forgetful and gives confused, out-of-context answers.

autoInjectGuardMinScore.description:
Minimum 0..1 score required before degraded HNSW fallback memories may auto-inject into the system prompt. Default: 0.86.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too low: Weak fallback memories can slip into the prompt and derail the current conversation. If too high: Legitimate fallback memories may be suppressed too aggressively.

anchorInjectionAssembles.description:
How many eligible prompt builds may temporarily inject the most recent compaction Anchor + summary. Default: 1. Budget-truncated early returns do not consume this window.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: Old compaction scaffolding lingers too long and starts crowding the prompt. If too low: The bridge text can disappear before the next prompt gets a chance to use it.

dedupWindow.description:
How many turns back the engine looks to block duplicate spam messages. Default: 5.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: The AI might wrongly ignore repeated but necessary commands. If too low: Your DB gets flooded with double-posts when the network lags.

maxBufferChars.description: Advanced: character threshold that forces the segmenter to flush regardless of surprise/time-gap boundaries. Default: 7200. Acts as a live flush guard to prevent unbounded buffer growth. ⚠️ Too high risks data loss on crash; too low causes I/O spam.

maxCharsPerChunk.description: Deprecated (legacy-only): max characters per chunk for the old batchIngest path. No longer used in the v0.4.x narrative cache path. Default: 9000. Retained for backward compatibility only.

segmentationLambda.description:
The AI's topic-shift sensitivity. Calculated as 'mean + (lambda * std)'. Default: 2.0.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: The AI never groups memories even when the topic completely changes. If too low: The AI becomes hyper-sensitive and slices the memory pointlessly just because you used a new word.

segmentationWarmupCount.description:
How many messages the AI needs to observe to learn your 'chat style' before using dynamic sensitivity. Default: 20.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: The AI stays in a dumb 'fixed threshold' mode for way too long. If too low: It finishes learning on skewed data and its slicing logic gets permanently bugged.

segmentationMinRawSurprise.description:
The absolute minimum 'Surprise Score' required to authorize cutting a new memory boundary.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: Slow, natural topic shifts are never saved and become one giant blob. If too low: The AI saves 'Um, okay' as completely separate, spammy memories.

segmentationCooldownTurns.description:
Mandatory wait turns after slicing a memory before the AI is allowed to slice again.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too long: Fast-paced topic changes get merged into high-speed chaos. If too short: The memory slicing misfires back-to-back, creating a hellish spam of 1-turn memories.

segmentationStdFloor.description:
A safety net (Standard Deviation floor) to prevent the AI from becoming hyper-sensitive during boring chats.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too low: During a boring chat, dropping a slightly interesting word causes the AI to overreact and snap the memory in half.

segmentationFallbackThreshold.description:
The fixed threshold used when the AI hasn't learned your style yet (Warmup) or encounters an error.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: Early conversations never get sliced. If too low: Early conversations get minced into dust.

recallSemanticFloor.description:
Recall search limit: Any memory with a vector similarity score below this is thrown in the trash.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: The AI becomes a perfectionist and throws away useful memories, recalling nothing. If too low: It pulls up irrelevant garbage memories, causing hallucination (lying).

recallUsefulnessClamp.description:
The maximum 'bonus points' a past memory can receive for being useful previously.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: One old, viral memory hogs the top spot forever, burying new important memories (Echo Chamber effect). If too low: The AI can't learn from past successes.

recallReplayTieBreakMaxBoost.description:
A tiny pity bonus given to old memories that haven't been reviewed in a while (Spaced Repetition).
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: Dead, useless memories hijack the search results simply because they are 'old'.

recallReplayLowRetrievabilityBonus.description:
The final desperate point boost given to a memory right before it gets permanently forgotten (decayed).
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: Garbage memories get zombified and refuse to die, turning your vector DB into a slum.

recallTopicsMatchBoost.description:
Bonus points awarded when your search perfectly matches the memory's tags/topics.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: The AI blindly grabs irrelevant memories just because the tags match, acting completely stupid.

recallTopicsMismatchPenalty.description:
Point penalty given to a memory that has tags, but completely missed your search criteria.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: A crucial memory with slightly mismatched tags gets locked in the DB dungeon forever.

recallTopicsMissingPenalty.description:
Point penalty for memories that have ZERO tags (Recommended: 0).
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: All your old 'tagless' ace memories get blacklisted and excluded from search.

sharedEpisodesDir.description: Disabled and ignored. Shared episode directories are no longer used at runtime.

allowCrossAgentRecall.description: Disabled and ignored. Cross-agent recall is no longer used at runtime.

tombstoneRetentionDays.description:
How many days to wait before physically deleting forgotten (Tombstoned) memories from the disk. Default: 14.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too short: You have zero time to salvage an important memory that got accidentally forgotten. If too long: You waste hard drive space endlessly.

enableBackgroundWorkers.description:
Enables background maintenance workers: HealingWorker (auto-rebuilds lexical/vector indexes, handles embedding 429 recovery) and replay scheduling. Default: true.
⚠️ If false: Index auto-repair and embedding rate-limit recovery are disabled. Narrative generation continues normally.

lexicalPreFilterLimit.description:
The max number of memories the lightning-fast Lexical (N-gram) engine filters before passing them to the heavy HNSW vector math. Default: 1000.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: Your CPU burns up attempting heavy vector math on the entire database. If too low: Excellent memories get casually thrown out by the dumb text-filter, ruining search accuracy.

recallReInjectionCooldownTurns.description:
Minimum total message turns (user + assistant) that must pass before the same recalled episode set may be re-injected into the system prompt. Default: 24 (≈12 user + 12 assistant exchanges). Set to 0 to disable.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: Freshly relevant memories won't reappear even when the conversation naturally circles back. If too low: The same memories spam the prompt every turn, wasting tokens and confusing the agent.

lexicalRebuildIntervalDays.description:
How often the HealingWorker checks for gaps in the Lexical (Bleve) index and auto-rebuilds if >10% of records are missing. Default: 7 days.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too short: Unnecessary background CPU usage. If too long: Lexical search may be degraded for weeks after a migration or corruption.

queryExcludedKeywords.description: Keywords to exclude from recall queries. Add words here to prevent them from being used in memory search (e.g., common noise words or sensitive topics).

recallQueryRecentMessageCount.description:
How many recent user messages are used to build the deterministic recall query (Parse/Rewrite window size, NOT raw latest message). Default: 4.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too high: Query becomes noisy and dilutes semantic intent. If too low: Insufficient context for accurate memory retrieval.

openrouterApiKey.description: OpenRouter API key for narrative episode generation. Falls back to OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var. Leave empty to disable narrative mode.

openrouterConfig.description: Configuration for the OpenRouter narrative generation model. Nested fields take precedence over flat equivalents.

maxTokens.description: Optional. Max tokens cap for the narrative summary. Omit to let OpenRouter/model decide (recommended).

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for narrative generation. Default: 0.4 (factual, consistent).

reasoning.description: OpenRouter reasoning/thinking control for narrative generation. Default: enabled=true, effort=high (output quality prioritized). Adjust to low/medium/none if latency or token cost is a concern.

enabled.description: Enable reasoning/thinking mode. Default: true. Set false to disable reasoning entirely.

effort.description: Reasoning effort level. Default: high. Higher effort may increase latency and token usage but improve output quality.

maxTokens.description: Optional. Max reasoning tokens cap. When set alongside effort, maxTokens takes precedence and effort is dropped.

exclude.description: When true, sends reasoning.exclude=true to request the model to exclude reasoning output from the response. Default: false.

narrativeSystemPrompt.description: Custom system prompt for narrative generation. Inline text or path to .md/.txt file. Leave empty for default.

narrativeUserPromptTemplate.description: Custom user prompt template. Variables: {previousEpisode}, {conversationText}. Inline text or path to .md/.txt file.

maxPoolChars.description: Advanced: maximum characters accumulated in the narrative pool before triggering a flush to the cache queue. Default: 15000. Acts as a pool flush guard to prevent context overflow. ⚠️ Too high risks model input limits; too low causes episode fragmentation.

narrativePreviousEpisodeRef.description: Pass the full previous episode to the LLM for context continuity. Default: true.

segmentationTimeGapMinutes.description:
Time gap threshold (minutes) between user messages that triggers an automatic segment boundary. Default: 15.
⚠️ [Blast Radius] If too short: Excessive segmentation breaks conversation flow. If too long: Long pauses don't create natural episode boundaries.
